features.summary: NVIDIA reported record Q4 2024 revenue of $22.1 billion, up 22% sequentially and 265% year-over-year, driven primarily by data center revenue of $18.4 billion (up 409% YoY). Full-year revenue reached $60.9 billion, up 126% from prior year. Data center growth was fueled by strong demand for Hopper GPU computing platform and InfiniBand networking across training and inference workloads. The company estimates approximately 40% of data center revenue came from AI inference, representing a significant shift from CPU-based to GPU-accelerated recommender systems and generative AI applications.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong growth through calendar 2024-2025, driven by two major industry transitions: the shift from general-purpose to accelerated computing and the emergence of generative AI. Q1 2025 revenue guidance is $24 billion (±2%), with sequential growth in data center and ProViz offset by seasonal gaming decline. The company anticipates next-generation products (Blackwell) will be supply constrained due to ramping from zero to large volumes. Management believes these trends will drive doubling of global data center infrastructure over the next five years, representing hundreds of billions in annual market opportunity.
features.risks: Supply constraints are expected to continue for next-generation products despite overall supply improvements, with demand significantly exceeding supply capabilities. China data center revenue declined significantly following October export controls, now representing mid-single digit percentage of data center revenue. Gross margins are expected to normalize to mid-70s range after Q1, down from current 76-77% levels that benefited from favorable component costs. The company faces allocation challenges across competing customers and must balance fair distribution while avoiding unnecessary inventory buildup.
features.key_drivers: Data center growth is driven by diverse end markets including hyperscale cloud providers (>50% of data center revenue), consumer internet companies, enterprise software platforms, and vertical industries like automotive ($1B+ data center revenue), healthcare, and financial services. Sovereign AI initiatives are emerging as countries invest in domestic AI infrastructure and language models. The software and services business reached $1 billion annualized run rate, with NVIDIA AI Enterprise positioned as an AI operating system at $4,500 per GPU annually. Gaming revenue of $2.87 billion was supported by RTX 40 Super Series launch and AI-enabled PC applications.
